definition: The equity-bond correlation flips from negative (-0.3 to -0.4) to positive (+0.2 to +0.4) when inflation persistently exceeds ~2.5%, destroying 60/40 and risk parity diversification assumptions calibrated to the 2010-2020 covariance matrix. Identified as the single most consequential parameter change for multi-asset portfolios.
evidence: Post-GFC calibration assumed negative equity-bond correlation under core PCE ~1.5-1.8%
When inflation persistently exceeds 2.5%, correlation averages +0.2 to +0.4
pair_2 debate affirmed as 'the single most consequential parameter change' for multi-asset portfolios
open_questions: What is the exact inflation threshold for the flip? (sample-dependent)
How quickly does the correlation regime shift after inflation crosses the threshold?
Are risk parity and mean-variance portfolios calibrated to 2010-2020 structurally mispriced if the floor has shifted to 2.3-2.8%?
